item.title: A retirement sendoff for a guardian angel
item.description: In 1965, Marco Houpe was just 15 months old when he was injured in a fire, with third-degree burns over 85% of his body. Wynola Wayne, then a young trainee nurse at her Columbus, Ohio hospital's burn unit, helped save his life. Recently, when Wayne retired after nearly six decades as a nurse, it was no ordinary farewell party, as Houpe made plain. Steve Hartman reports.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 19)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Passage: In memoriam
item.description: "Sunday Morning" remembers some of the notable figures who left us this week, including Oscar-winning actress Brenda Fricker ("My Left Foot"), and Sam Neill, star of the blockbuster "Jurassic Park."

item.title: Judge Ronald Gould on his MS: "Just take life a step at a time"
item.description: At 79, Ronald Gould, a federal judge on the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals in Seattle, shoulders a full caseload despite living with progressive multiple sclerosis – a condition he faces with grit and a healthy dose of humor.

item.title: Judge Ronald Gould on persevering despite his MS
item.description: Ronald Gould is a federal judge on the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals in Seattle, one rung below the U.S. Supreme Court. At 79, he shoulders a full caseload despite living with progressive multiple sclerosis. He talks with CBS News chief medical correspondent Dr. Jon LaPook about working with MS, a condition he faces with grit and a healthy dose of humor. LaPook also talks with one of Gould's former law clerks, Karla Gilbride, an attorney who has been blind since birth.

item.title: Ted Danson with 4 tips on aging creatively
item.description: Studies show that having a positive outlook about aging, and having good social networks, can have positive health benefits, from preventing memory loss to a longer life expectancy. Actor Ted Danson presents "Sunday Morning" viewers with advice on how to embrace aging.

item.title: Carol Bove's heavy metal artistry
item.description: Carol Bove's bent and warped steel sculptures tower above visitors to the artist's retrospective at New York's Guggenheim Museum. Correspondent Faith Salie talks with Bove about how her artwork invites museum-goers into an environment of curiosity and playfulness.

item.title: Pitch perfect: Developing natural grass for the World Cup
item.description: FIFA rules state that World Cup matches must be played on natural grass. For this year's tournament, scientifically-engineered sod (that can withstand playing conditions and differing climates) was deployed in stadiums with artificial turf.

item.title: Pitch perfect: Developing natural grass for the World Cup
item.description: FIFA rules state that World Cup matches must be played on pitches with natural grass. For this year's tournament, where some matches were scheduled in stadiums with artificial turf, special varieties of grass were required to withstand playing conditions and differing climates. Conor Knighton examines how scientifically-engineered sod was deployed, sometimes in domed stadiums, to ensure resiliency under the demands of World Cup competition.

item.title: Almanac: July 19
item.description: "Sunday Morning" looks back at historical events on this date.

item.title: What's wrong with Congress?
item.description: Congress was granted many powers under Article I of the Constitution. Nonetheless, lawmakers have ceded much of their authority to the president – and Washington has been plagued by gridlock.

item.title: What's the matter with Congress?
item.description: Congress was granted many powers under Article I of the Constitution. Nonetheless, in recent decades, lawmakers – including under current GOP majorities – have ceded much of their authority to the president, while Washington has been plagued by gridlock, and a sense that Congress has been marginalized. As both parties point fingers, "Sunday Morning" national correspondent Robert Costa talks with Capitol Hill observers who discuss why the House and Senate are for the most part failing to address many problems facing Americans, and whether they are optimistic that Congress – beset by partisanship, gerrymandering, and the president's pressures to do his bidding – could one day find its way again.

item.title: U.S. concludes 8th night of strikes on Iran after Iranian attack kills 2 U.S. soldiers
item.description: In addition to the two U.S. service members killed, a third is missing in action, U.S. Central Command said.

item.title: Attorney Karla Gilbride on challenges facing people with disabilities
item.description: In this web exclusive, Karla Gilbride, an attorney who has been blind since birth, and who formerly served as general counsel of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, talked with CBS News chief medical correspondent Dr. Jon LaPook about what abled people should consider when thinking about people with disabilities.

item.title: Thunderstorms expected to clear Canadian wildfire smoke before World Cup final
item.description: Saturday's storm front will largely move the smoke out of the Northeast before the final between Spain and Argentina, said Tyler Roys, a senior meteorologist at AccuWeather.

item.title: U.S. citizen who was trapped in Iran for a year is home, lawyer says
item.description: A U.S.-Iranian woman who was trapped in Iran on allegations of espionage and collaborating with a hostile state that her attorney called "bogus" departed the country earlier this week.

item.title: Russia launches deadly, massive ballistic missile attack in Kyiv, Ukraine
item.description: One person was killed and 16 others wounded in the overnight Russian attack on Kyiv, local authorities said.

item.title: A look back at the 2022 FIFA World Cup final
item.description: As the 2026 World Cup draws to an end, take a look back at the memorable 2022 final match between Argentina and France.

item.title: How do Spain and Argentina stack up? World Cup final teams by the numbers.
item.description: Forty-eight nations​ competed in the largest FIFA World Cup in history and two are left standing, with Spain in search of its second World Cup title and Argentina shooting for back-to-back​ championships.

item.title: How to watch the 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup today
item.description: Spain will face Argentina in the 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup final match on July 19. Here's how and when to watch the soccer game.

item.title: Spain and Argentina's World Cup trophy count before 2026 final
item.description: When Spain and Argentina face off Sunday in the World Cup final, it will mark just the second meeting between the two sides in the tournament's history, and their first in 60 years.

item.title: California man charged in missing wife's murder left a review for spellcaster
item.description: Investigators say Larry Millete contacted spellcasters to try to put a hex on his wife Maya so she wouldn't leave him.

item.title: Timeline: The disappearance of Maya Millete
item.description: Maya Millete, 39, went missing in January 2021. What was happening between her and her husband before she disappeared and what does spellcasting have to do with it?

item.title: Police say husband asked spellcasters to hex his now missing wife
item.description: Shortly before Maya "May" Millete vanished, authorities say her husband Larry messaged a spellcaster, "Please punish May and incapacitate her enough so she can't leave the house."

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: Where's Maya Millete? Family, friends continue search for missing California mom
item.description: A California mother disappears without a trace – did her husband try to have a hex put on her so she wouldn't leave him?

item.title: Searching for Maya Millete
item.description: A young mother disappears. Investigators say her husband contacted spellcasters to put a hex on his wife so she wouldn't leave him. "48 Hours" contributor Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

item.title: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested by federal authorities Saturday in Miami.

item.title: Colorado ICE center employee arrested after allegedly shooting protester
item.description: An employee of a group that operates an ICE detainment center in Aurora, Colorado, was arrested after police said he shot and wounded a woman who was taking part in a protest.

item.title: Second Dan Sullivan can appear on Alaska Senate primary ballot, court rules
item.description: The Alaska Supreme Court ruled Monday that a man with the same name and party as Republican Sen. Dan Sullivan can challenge the sitting lawmaker in the state's Senate primary in August.
